relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The dwelling-place begins as woven beauty with cherubim.
Each curtain receives measured form.
Curtains join into ordered sets.
Blue loops prepare connection.
Loop meets loop across the seam.
Gold clasps make the dwelling one.
Goats' hair forms the tent over the dwelling.
The outer curtains have their measure.
The sets fold and shelter the front.
Loops prepare the second joining.
Bronze clasps make the tent one.
Surplus covers the back.
The tent hangs over the sides as covering.
Ram skins and durable hides protect the tent.
Acacia frames stand upright.
Each frame receives length and width.
Tenons join frame to base.
Twenty frames stand southward.
Silver bases hold the frames.
Twenty frames stand northward.
Silver bases hold the north side.
Six frames close the west.
Two frames strengthen the rear corners.
Corners are doubled into completion.
Eight rear frames rest on sixteen bases.
Acacia bars bind one side.
Bars bind the second side and rear.
The middle bar runs end to end.
Gold overlays frame, ring, and bar.
The mountain pattern governs the dwelling.
The veil is woven with color and cherubim.
Four gold-covered pillars hold the veil.
The veil separates Holy from Holy of Holies.
The cover rests upon the ark in the innermost place.
Table and lampstand face across the holy space.
The entrance screen is embroidered in sacred colors.
Five pillars and bronze bases hold the entry.

contemplative_note: Exodus 26 is the grammar of sacred space: many pieces joined into one dwelling, layered protection, guarded nearness, and beauty portable enough for wilderness.
